Name: Brown Boobook
Habitat: Well-wooded areas, tropical forests, large gardens
Best_seen_at: Western Ghats, Northeastern India, and Andaman Islands
How_to_identify: Hawk-like appearance with a small head and no distinct facial disk. Upperparts are dark chocolate brown; underparts are white with heavy reddish-brown vertical streaks. Bright yellow eyes.
Diet: Large insects, frogs, small lizards, birds, and mice

Name: Hume's Boobook
How_to_identify: Lacks the distinct streaking seen on the Brown Boobook. Entirely dark chocolate-brown plumage with a very faint or absent pattern on the belly. Yellow eyes and a pale cere.

Name: Andaman Boobook
How_to_identify: Smaller than other boobooks. Features heavily barred underparts rather than streaks. Upperparts are dark brown; throat is buffy-white. Distinct yellow eyes.

Name: Long-eared Owl
Habitat: Coniferous forests, wooded areas near open grasslands
How_to_identify: Identified by long, prominent vertical ear tufts located near the center of the head. Buff-colored facial disk with orange-yellow eyes. Body is heavily mottled with brown and buff.
Diet: Small mammals like voles and mice, occasionally birds

Name: Short-eared Owl
Best_seen_at: Velavadar Blackbuck National Park, Little Rann of Kutch
How_to_identify: Small, often invisible ear tufts. Pale facial disk with yellow eyes surrounded by black rings. Often hunts during the day; flight is jerky and moth-like.
Diet: Rodents, small mammals, and occasionally insects

Name: Malabar Trogon
How_to_identify: Males have a black head/throat separated from a crimson breast by a white band. Females have a dark brown head and orange-yellow underparts. Both have long, broad tails.
Diet: Insects, caterpillars, and occasionally small fruits

Name: Red-headed Trogon
Habitat: Subtropical and tropical moist montane forests
Best_seen_at: Northeastern India (Mishmi Hills, Namdapha National Park)
How_to_identify: Males feature a brilliant deep red head and underparts with a brown back and white tail borders. Females have a brown head and upper breast with pinkish-red lower underparts.

Name: Ward's Trogon
Habitat: Broadleaved evergreen forests with bamboo undergrowth
How_to_identify: A large trogon. Males have a maroon-red back and pinkish-red forehead and underparts. Females are unique with a yellowish-green head and yellow underparts.

Name: Eurasian Hoopoe
Habitat: Open woodland, cultivation, lawns, and gardens
How_to_identify: Highly distinctive fan-like crest with black tips, usually held flat but raised when excited. Body is salmon-pink/cinnamon with bold black-and-white barred wings and tail.
Diet: Insects, larvae, and pupae probed from the ground

Name: Great Hornbill
Habitat: Primary evergreen and moist deciduous forests
Best_seen_at: Western Ghats and Himalayan foothills (Jim Corbett, Kaziranga)
How_to_identify: Massive bird with a large, yellow-and-black U-shaped casque atop a large bill. Body is mostly black with a white neck, white wing bars, and a white tail with a black subterminal band.
Diet: Mainly fruits (especially figs), also small mammals, birds, and reptiles
